seo.title: Multi-Agent Coordination Failure in Haystack Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix multi-agent coordination failure in Haystack agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Haystack, Multi-Agent Coordination Failure occurs when agents in a pipeline—such as retriever, reader, and generator—fail to synchronize outputs, leading to type mismatches or context loss between components. This manifests as pipelines silently failing when one agent's output schema doesn't match the next agent's input expectations, or when parallel agents produce inconsistent results without proper merging. These failures cascade through composable pipelines, breaking end-to-end reliability in production RAG systems.

howItManifests.scenario: Retriever outputs Document objects but Reader expects DocumentWithScore
howItManifests.symptom: Pipeline runs without errors but reader receives empty input, returning None
howItManifests.impact: Zero answers generated despite relevant documents being retrieved

howItManifests.scenario: Parallel reranking agents return different document formats
howItManifests.symptom: MergeDocuments join fails with AttributeError on missing fields
howItManifests.impact: Pipeline crashes mid-execution, requiring manual format alignment

howItManifests.scenario: Generator agent ignores retrieved context metadata
howItManifests.symptom: Answers lack citations despite Document objects containing metadata
howItManifests.impact: Users receive hallucinated responses without traceability

howItManifests.scenario: Custom evaluation agent swallows exceptions in async execution
howItManifests.symptom: Pipeline completes successfully but evaluation metrics are all zeros
howItManifests.impact: Degraded performance goes undetected in production monitoring

detection.name: Pipeline Type Mismatch Scanner
detection.description: Inspects connected components for output/input schema compatibility using Haystack's type annotations

detection.name: Agent Output Consistency Check
detection.description: Validates that parallel agents produce consistent output schemas during test runs

detection.name: Silent Failure Monitor
detection.description: Detects components that complete without errors but produce empty/null outputs

fixes.name: Type Conversion Wrapper
fixes.description: Automatically converts Document to DocumentWithScore for compatible chaining
fixes.code:
from haystack.components.converters import DocumentToDocumentWithScore
from haystack import Pipeline

# Fix type mismatch between retriever and reader
pipe = Pipeline()
pipe.add_components([
    in_retriever,
    DocumentToDocumentWithScore(),
    reader
])

pipe.connect('in_retriever.documents', 'document_to_document_with_score.documents')
pipe.connect('document_to_document_with_score.document_with_score', 'reader.documents')

# Run with proper typing
result = pipe.run({'in_retriever': {'query': 'your query'}})

fixes.name: Standardized Merge Operator
fixes.description: Merges parallel agent outputs with consistent schema enforcement
fixes.code:
from haystack.components.builders import MergeDocuments

# Fix inconsistent parallel agent outputs
merge_op = MergeDocuments(merge_strategy='concatenate')

pipe = Pipeline()
pipe.add_components([
    agent1_reranker,
    agent2_reranker,
    merge_op
])

pipe.connect('agent1.documents', 'merge.documents_1')
pipe.connect('agent2.documents', 'merge.documents_2')

# Ensures consistent Document schema post-merge
result = pipe.run({
    'agent1': {'query': 'test'},
    'agent2': {'query': 'test'}
})

fixes.name: Context-Preserving Generator
fixes.description: Forces generator to include retrieved context metadata in responses
fixes.code:
from haystack.components.generators import OpenAIGenerator
from haystack.dataclasses import ChatMessage

class ContextAwareGenerator(OpenAIGenerator):
    def run(self, **kwargs):
        documents = kwargs.get('documents', [])
        context = '\n'.join([f"[DOC:{i}] {doc.content}" for i, doc in enumerate(documents)])
        
        prompt = ChatMessage.from_user(
            f"Answer using only this context:\n{context}\n\nQuestion: {kwargs.get('query')}\nInclude [DOC:#] citations."
        )
        
        kwargs['messages'] = [prompt]
        kwargs.pop('documents', None)
        kwargs.pop('query', None)
        
        return super().run(**kwargs)

# Usage
context_gen = ContextAwareGenerator(model='gpt-4o-mini')
pipe.add_component('context_gen', context_gen)

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Multi-Agent Coordination Failure Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates as a custom Haystack component that automatically monitors pipeline execution for coordination failures across all agents. It captures type mismatches, empty outputs, and schema inconsistencies in real-time, providing structured failure reports with remediation suggestions. Vex's agent runs parallel to your main pipeline, validating outputs against expected schemas defined in PipelineSchema.

The Vex component uses Haystack's observability hooks to trace data flow between agents, flagging issues like missing metadata propagation or inconsistent document formats. When failures are detected, Vex can automatically apply fixes like type conversion or output normalization before passing data to downstream components. This ensures production pipelines remain resilient even when agent coordination breaks.

Integration requires only adding the VexMonitor component and defining expected schemas once. Vex handles the continuous validation, alerting, and auto-remediation, reducing MTTR from hours to seconds.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex_sdk.haystack import VexMonitor
from haystack import Pipeline
from haystack.components.builders import PipelineSchema

# Install: pip install haystack-ai vex-sdk

# 1. Define expected pipeline schema
schema = PipelineSchema([
    {'retriever': {'documents': 'List[Document]'}},
    {'reader': {'documents': 'List[DocumentWithScore]'}},
    {'generator': {'documents': 'List[Document]', 'query': 'str'}}
])

# 2. Add Vex as monitoring component
vex_monitor = VexMonitor(schema=schema, api_key='your-vex-api-key')

pipe = Pipeline()
pipe.add_components([
    retriever, reader, generator, vex_monitor
])

# 3. Auto-connect with validation
pipe.connect('retriever.documents', 'vex_monitor.validate_retriever')
pipe.connect('reader.document_with_score', 'vex_monitor.validate_reader')
pipe.connect('vex_monitor.validated_output', 'generator.documents')

# 4. Vex auto-detects and fixes coordination failures
result = pipe.run({'retriever': {'query': 'What is Haystack?'}})

# Vex reports
print(vex_monitor.get_failure_report())

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add Vex to your Haystack pipelines today and eliminate multi-agent coordination failures automatically.
